channel.title: NPR Topics: Economy
channel.description: NPR news on the U.S. and world economy, the World Bank, and Federal Reserve. Commentary on economic trends. Subscribe to NPR Economy podcasts and RSS feeds.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: In first Congressional testimony, new Fed Chief Warsh vows to fight inflation
item.description: Kevin Warsh testified before the Senate Banking Committee for the first time Wednesday since taking over as chairman of the Federal Reserve. Warsh pledged to bring inflation under control.
item.content:encoded: Kevin Warsh testified before the Senate Banking Committee for the first time Wednesday since taking over as chairman of the Federal Reserve. Warsh pledged to bring inflation under control.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: China's economy grows 4.3% in Q2, slowest since late 2022
item.description: Lagging consumer spending and business investment offset the boost from strong exports thanks partly to the boom in artificial intelligence.
item.content:encoded:
Lagging consumer spending and business investment offset the boost from strong exports thanks partly to the boom in artificial intelligence.

(Image credit: AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: What a Monopoly importer learned when it tried to make things in the U.S.A.
item.description: After getting hit with a steep tariff bill for the imported board games he sells, Jonathan Silva wanted to see if he could produce a version of his Monopoly game in the United States.
item.content:encoded: After getting hit with a steep tariff bill for the imported board games he sells, Jonathan Silva wanted to see if he could produce a version of his Monopoly game in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Inflation has been creeping up. How are Americans coping?
item.description: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with three people about how they're feeling the effects of inflation: Jennifer Browning, Alex Garcia and Chuck Lockhart.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with three people about how they're feeling the effects of inflation: Jennifer Browning, Alex Garcia and Chuck Lockhart.
item.dc:creator: Kai McNamee

item.title: What a Monopoly importer learned when it tried to make things in the U.S.A.
item.description: After getting hit with tariffs for the imported board games he sells, Jonathan Silva decided to see if he could produce a version of his Monopoly game in the United States. This is what he learned.
item.content:encoded:
After getting hit with tariffs for the imported board games he sells, Jonathan Silva decided to see if he could produce a version of his Monopoly game in the United States. This is what he learned.

(Image credit: WS Game Company)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Even Taco Tuesday could be more expensive as beef prices continue to rise
item.description: Beef prices are continuing to rise due to drought, low herd sizes and concerns about the new world screwworm in the United States, but business owners in Texas say that raising prices could drive away customers.
item.content:encoded:
Beef prices are continuing to rise due to drought, low herd sizes and concerns about the new world screwworm in the United States, but business owners in Texas say that raising prices could drive away customers.

(Image credit: Danielle Villasana for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Natalie Weber

item.title: How much has the Iran war cost U.S. households?
item.description: NPR's A Martinez speaks with Mark Zandi, chief economist at Moody's Analytics, who has estimated what the Iran war has cost the average U.S. household so far.
item.content:encoded: NPR's A Martinez speaks with Mark Zandi, chief economist at Moody's Analytics, who has estimated what the Iran war has cost the average U.S. household so far.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh isn't as focused on employment, part of the dual mandate
item.description: The Federal Reserve has two main goals: price stability and maximum employment. But new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh seems to be leaning into price stability and away from full employment as equal goals.
item.content:encoded: The Federal Reserve has two main goals: price stability and maximum employment. But new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh seems to be leaning into price stability and away from full employment as equal goals.
item.dc:creator: Wailin Wong

item.title: Oil and gasoline prices begin to climb after Trump says ceasefire with Iran is over
item.description: Oil and gasoline prices are climbing again after President Trump declared an end to the ceasefire with Iran. That could put more upward pressure on inflation.
item.content:encoded: Oil and gasoline prices are climbing again after President Trump declared an end to the ceasefire with Iran. That could put more upward pressure on inflation.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Tensions with Iran add fresh uncertainty to an already shaky global economy
item.description: Crude oil prices jumped and stock prices fell after President Trump declared an end to the ceasefire with Iran, adding fresh uncertainty to an already shaky outlook for the global economy.
item.content:encoded:
Crude oil prices jumped and stock prices fell after President Trump declared an end to the ceasefire with Iran, adding fresh uncertainty to an already shaky outlook for the global economy.

(Image credit: Michael M. Santiago)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley
